info.name: Attendify Administrative API
info.description: Administrative workspace endpoints for Attendify. New administrative access is granted to doctor accounts through `administrative_access=true`; direct creation of new standalone administrative accounts is disabled. Legacy standalone administrative accounts may still exist and remain supported. When the rank is enabled, the same authenticated user and token can access both the doctor and administrative workspaces. Check `administrative_access` and `available_workspaces` in auth responses.

request.description: Authenticate into the administrative workspace. This can be a legacy standalone administrative account or a doctor account that was granted `administrative_access=true`. New setups should use doctor + administrative_access. When the current account can use both workspaces, auth responses should expose `available_workspaces` containing doctor and administrative.

Return the current administrative profile and workspace flags. Use this request to confirm whether the account is a legacy standalone administrative user or a doctor with administrative rank by checking `administrative_access` and `available_workspaces`.
Update college-level attendance settings. excuse_receiver accepts administrative or doctor, and qr_rotation_seconds controls QR token rotation and expiry.
Create a college broadcast. When `target=doctors`, the announcement is delivered to all doctor accounts in the current college.
Lists excuses for this college. Each excuse now includes all uploaded attachments, including legacy single-file excuses and new attachments[].
Review an excuse only when this college routes excuses to the administrative office. resolution values: excused_permission, excused_exemption, keep_absent.
Directly update a single attendance record. Allowed statuses are present, absent, late, permitted, and exempted.
Returns attendance analytics with separate counts for present, absent, late, permitted, exempted, and the aggregate excused_total.
term_id must belong to the same college and selected level. Use the majors endpoint to inspect levels and the subject/exam helper endpoints to resolve valid academic ids before creating records.

Use a real attendance record id from the attendance report endpoints. The status must be one of the editable attendance states configured by the backend, such as present, absent, late, permitted, or exempted.
Use create-data and helper endpoints first to resolve a valid major_id, level_id, term_id, and subject_id combination. A hard-coded term outside the selected level will correctly fail validation.

body.raw:
{
  "major_id": {{major_id}},
  "level_id": {{level_id}},
  "term_id": {{term_id}},
  "title": "Midterm Schedule Updated",
  "description": "Updated spring exam schedule",
  "is_published": true,
  "items": [
    {
      "subject_id": {{subject_id}},
      "exam_date": "2026-05-03",
      "start_time": "10:00",
      "end_time": "12:00",
      "location": "Hall B"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Create an academic schedule row. hall_name is optional and can be null.
Update an academic schedule row. hall_name remains optional.
